description_long: This object is a small, boxy airplane with a rectangular fuselage and a flat, elongated wing structure extending from either side. The fuselage appears metallic with a greenish tint, featuring visible panel lines and what seem to be two rectangular windows or hatches on the top. The wings are straight and attached at a low position relative to the fuselage, painted in a solid dark blue color with a smooth, unadorned surface. The tail section is not distinctly visible, but the overall design suggests a utilitarian, possibly cargo-oriented aircraft. The landing gear is not clearly visible, but the undercarriage appears to have a recessed area, possibly for retractable gear. The airplane's design is simple and functional, with minimal decorative elements and a focus on geometric shapes.
description: A small, boxy airplane with a metallic fuselage and long, straight dark blue wings, featuring a utilitarian and geometric design.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wide, flat blue wings and the metallic fuselage with visible panel lines and two top hatches.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thin profile of the blue wing and the rectangular, greenish fuselage with top windows.
description_view_2: Top-down view dominated by the solid dark blue wingspan, with little detail visible on the fuselage.
description_view_3: Opposite side view similar to view 1, showing the wing's thinness and the boxy fuselage with top features.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing configuration with straight, rectangular wings and no visible winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: No visible engines present on the fuselage or wings.
attribute_tail_design: Tail section not clearly visible; no distinct vertical or horizontal stabilizer features apparent.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Landing gear not clearly visible; possible recessed or retractable configuration suggested by undercarriage shape.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Cockpit appears to be forward-mounted and enclosed, with two rectangular windows or hatches on the top of the fuselage.
